subject: Logical Reasoning
question_text:
Statements:
I. All birds are wings.
II. All wings are flying objects.

Conclusions:
I. All birds are flying objects.
II. Some flying objects are birds.

explanation.concept: Standard Syllogism (All A are B, All B are C -> All A are C)
step_by_step: From Statement I and II: Birds ⊂ Wings ⊂ Flying Objects.
Since Birds are a subset of Wings, and Wings are a subset of Flying Objects, all Birds are Flying Objects (Conclusion I follows).
Since Birds are a part of the Flying Objects set, some Flying Objects are definitely Birds (Conclusion II follows).

subject: Logical Reasoning
question_text:
Statements:
I. Some pens are pencils.
II. All pencils are erasers.

Conclusions:
I. All erasers are pencils.
II. Some erasers are pens.

step_by_step: Statement II says All Pencils ⊂ Erasers. This does not mean All Erasers ⊂ Pencils. So Conclusion I fails.
Some Pens overlap with Pencils (Stmt I), and all Pencils are inside Erasers (Stmt II). Therefore, the Pens that are Pencils must also be Erasers. So Conclusion II follows.

subject: Logical Reasoning
question_text:
Statements:
I. No cat is a dog.
II. No dog is a lion.

Conclusions:
I. No cat is a lion.
II. Some lions are cats.

explanation.concept: Negative Syllogism (No A is B, No B is C)
step_by_step: Cat and Dog are disjoint. Dog and Lion are disjoint.
However, this does not give any direct information about the relationship between Cat and Lion.
They could be disjoint, overlapping, or one could be a subset of the other.
Therefore, neither 'No' nor 'Some' can be concluded definitely.

subject: Logical Reasoning
question_text:
Statements:
I. All apples are red.
II. Some red items are fruits.

Conclusions:
I. Some apples are fruits.
II. Some fruits are red.

step_by_step: All apples are inside the 'red' circle. Some 'red' circle overlaps with 'fruits'.
There is no guarantee that the overlap of 'red' and 'fruits' includes the 'apples' section. So Conclusion I is not definite.
Conclusion II is simply a restatement of Statement II (If some red are fruits, some fruits are red). So it follows.

subject: Logical Reasoning
question_text:
Statements:
I. Only a few students are dancers.
II. No dancer is a singer.

Conclusions:
I. Some students are not singers.
II. All students being singers is a possibility.

step_by_step: 'Only a few students are dancers' means Some students are dancers AND Some students are NOT dancers.

Since some students are definitely dancers, and no dancer is a singer, those specific students who are dancers cannot be singers. Thus, 'Some students are not singers' is definitely true (Conclusion I follows).
Because some students MUST be dancers and dancers CANNOT be singers, all students can NEVER be singers. Thus, Conclusion II is false.

subject: Logical Reasoning
question_text:
Statements:
I. All tables are chairs.
II. No chair is a bench.

Conclusions:
I. All tables being benches is a possibility.
II. No table is a bench.

step_by_step: Tables ⊂ Chairs. Chairs and Benches are disjoint (No Chair is Bench).
Therefore, anything inside the 'Chairs' circle (Tables) can never touch the 'Bench' circle.
No Table is Bench is definitely true. The possibility of them overlapping is zero.

subject: Logical Reasoning
question_text:
Statements:
I. All windows are doors.
II. Some doors are walls.

Conclusions:
I. Some windows are walls.
II. No window is a wall.

step_by_step: Both conclusions are not definitely true individually based on the statements.
However, they form a 'Some' + 'No' pair with the same elements (windows and walls).
In any scenario, either there will be at least one window that is a wall, or there will be no window that is a wall. Thus, it's an 'Either-Or' case.

subject: Logical Reasoning
question_text:
Statements:
I. No plastic is wood.
II. Some wood is metal.

Conclusions:
I. Some metal is not plastic.
II. All plastic being metal is a possibility.

step_by_step: The part of Metal that is Wood can never be Plastic (because No Wood is Plastic). So 'Some Metal is not Plastic' is true (Conclusion I follows).
There is no restriction on Plastic being a subset of the 'Metal' circle, as long as it doesn't touch the 'Wood' part. So 'All plastic being metal' is a possibility (Conclusion II follows).

subject: Logical Reasoning
question_text:
Statements:
I. Every ocean is water.
II. 0% water is land.

Conclusions:
I. All oceans are land.
II. No ocean is land.

step_by_step: 'Every ocean is water' = All oceans are water.
'0% water is land' = No water is land.
If no water is land, and all oceans are inside water, then no ocean can be land.
